$comment: Source of truth for the apodictic:argument_spine block (Nonfiction Pre-Draft Pathway, Increment 1). The PRE-writing plan of an argument — produced before a draft exists — that SEEDS the shared Argument_State.md artifact (docs/argument-state-schema.md): `thesis` -> §2 C0 (main claim); `subclaims` -> §2 claim ladder; `anti_thesis` -> §6 Objection 1 (the opposing view the argument must defeat); the §1 classification fields (form, goal, argument_type, burden_level, audience_*). The draft-dependent sections (§§3-5, 7-9) are NOT planned here — the Dialectical Clarity audit fills them once a draft exists. Enums mirror Argument_State §1/§2: argument_type AT0-AT4; burden_level LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH; audience_expertise GENERAL/MIXED/EXPERT; audience_receptivity SYMPATHETIC/MIXED/HOSTILE; stakes_type CONSEQUENTIAL/MORAL/EPISTEMIC/PRACTICAL. The Firewall holds: the spine plans argument STRUCTURE; it does not invent claims or write prose. Validated by scripts/argument_spine.py, which also checks the spine actually seeded Argument_State (A2/A3). See docs/nonfiction-pre-draft.md.
title: APODICTIC argument spine (apodictic.argument_spine.v1)
